paper_title: Grain Size and Age Model Analysis in Arctic Sediments

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the grain size distribution of sediment samples to understand sedimentary processes and environmental conditions. The process includes sieving, drying, and analyzing samples using a Sedigraph to determine silt statistical parameters.

steps.description: A continuous set of 2 cm samples was taken from a u-channel.
Sub-samples (3-4 g) of these were wet sieved through a 63-mm mesh.
The sieving liquid was deionized water saturated with CaCO3 to preserve calcareous microfossils.
steps.searchable_summary: use CaCO3 saturated water for sieving

steps.description: The coarse fraction was dried on the mesh in an oven and allowed to cool before weighing.
The fine fraction was collected in beakers, allowed to settle, water siphoned out, and samples centrifuged.
steps.searchable_summary: settle, siphon, and centrifuge fine fraction

steps.description: A dispersion liquid (NaPO3) was added to the samples and they were thoroughly suspended using sonification.
Each sample was analysed using 0.08 phi bin size divisions for the 1-63 mm fraction in the Sedigraph 5100.
Silt statistical parameters (mean, sorting, skewness, and kurtosis) were determined using the GRADISTAT software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate silt statistical parameters with GRADISTAT

methods.description: This method establishes a stratigraphic correlation with the ACEX composite section to map the Quaternary age model onto Core 18, using various geological and magnetic parameters.

steps.description: A stratigraphic correlation with the composite section from the Integrated Ocean Drilling Programs Expedition 302 was established using bulk density and magnetic susceptibility records.
steps.searchable_summary: establish stratigraphic correlation using bulk density and magnetic susceptibility

steps.description: The stratigraphic correlation allows the Quaternary age model presented for the ACEX record to be mapped onto Core 18.
steps.searchable_summary: map Quaternary age model onto Core 18

steps.description: Beyond the last two glacial cycles, the ACEX age model was derived from cyclostratigraphic analysis of physical and environmental magnetic parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: derive age model from cyclostratigraphic analysis
